artifacts.summary: Created 3 new metalogic tasks with correct dependencies, confirmed existing tasks need no revision, updated ROADMAP.md with full metalogic layer

completion_data.completion_summary: Created 3 new tasks (29: Generic MCS Foundations, 30: Modal Metalogic, 31: Temporal Metalogic) with full standalone scope (DT + MCS + Soundness + Completeness) and correct dependencies. Updated task 7 to depend on task 29. Updated ROADMAP.md with Phase 4 (Standalone Metalogic), revised import hierarchy, component accounting, and success metrics.

memory_candidates.content: When structuring metalogic across multiple logics: ~60% of MCS theory (SetConsistent, SetMaximalConsistent, Lindenbaum skeleton, consistent_chain_union, closed_under_derivation) is generic and can be parameterized over an abstract derivation relation. The remaining ~40% (negation_complete, witness conditions) depends on per-logic deduction theorems and cannot be genericized. Do not create HasDeductionTheorem typeclasses — each logic's metalogic should be standalone importing only Foundations generic definitions.

Metalogic import hierarchy for CSLib: Foundations/Logic/Metalogic/ (generic MCS) -> Modal/Metalogic/ (Task 30) and Temporal/Metalogic/ (Task 31) independently -> Bimodal/Metalogic/ (Task 7, also imports Task 29). Modal and Temporal metalogic never import from each other. Bimodal metalogic imports generic MCS from Foundations but has its own per-logic DeductionTheorem.
